purpose: Defines the highest-leverage path for Buddy to compete as a trust-first AI company, bot, media, course, game, and simulation builder.
strategy: Do not compete only on faster code generation. Compete on governed one-prompt delivery, client trust, bot business packaging, live proof, and automatic quality gates.

phases.title: Win the trust lane first
phases.goal: Make Buddy the safest way to build client-ready apps, bots, media packets, courses, games, and simulations.

phases.title: Ship one-prompt build packets
phases.goal: Turn one client prompt into plan, files, sandbox preview, tests, rollback notes, and prospectus page.

phases.title: Own bot businesses, not just apps
phases.goal: Package every build as an AI employee, department, or company with tools, APIs, webhooks, memory, and dashboards.

phases.title: Add live preview and visual revision
phases.goal: Let clients watch Buddy build and revise layouts, scenes, lessons, screenshots, and game screens from preview feedback.

phases.title: Bundle essential business services
phases.goal: Offer safe starter paths for auth, database, storage, payments, email, webhooks, and deployment.

phases.title: Make quality automatic
phases.goal: Run evaluations after every generated change and block risky releases until evidence is clean.

first_30_days: Make one-prompt build packets reliable for bot systems and dashboard demos.
Add live preview status, screenshots, and test evidence to the Actions page.
Create starter templates for app, bot, learning video packet, course, game, and simulation.
Add service templates for auth, database, storage, email, payments, and webhooks in sandbox mode.
Keep all media cloning and live-money actions blocked until consent and owner approval are recorded.
first_90_days: Turn the best templates into repeatable client packages.
Add guided visual editing and screenshot-to-dashboard generation.
Add deploy targets with rollback and approval evidence.
Track cost, quality, client handoff time, and test pass rate for every build.
Use Buddy as the public face of trust, not just the build assistant.
release_rule: Buddy wins by being safer, more complete, and more business-ready than generic prompt-to-code tools.
